name: lifters-skills
description: AI-native product development skills for Lifters teams
author: twinfo-io

skills.name: lf-discovery
skills.description: Interactive feature discovery for product development teams. Conducts a structured 7-phase interview, collects reference documents, researches market benchmarks, and generates a discovery.md artifact.

skills.name: lf-briefing-ux
skills.description: Generates the UX/UI briefing from an existing discovery.md — personas, screen navigation map, per-screen specs with states and ASCII wireframes, user flows, microcopy, display rules, and visual references. Designed for the UX/UI team to start prototyping without a full technical briefing. Use after /lf-discovery and before /lf-new-feature.

skills.name: lf-new-feature
skills.description: Generates the technical briefing (briefing-tech.vN.md, 15 sections) from an existing discovery.md. Can be called multiple times to iterate versions (v0 → v1 → v2). If briefing-ux.vN.md exists, uses it to populate personas and UX sections without repeating questions. Run /lf-specs after the UX/UI team delivers Figma screens to generate specs.md and wps.md.

skills.name: lf-specs
skills.description: Generates specs.md and wps.md after the UX/UI team delivers Figma screens. Reads the latest briefing-tech.vN.md, interactively collects one Figma URL per screen, fetches design context via Figma MCP, creates briefing-tech.v(N+1).md with Section 16 (Figma references) and inline screen links, then generates specs.md (SPEC-XX per domain with Figma URLs inline) and wps.md (work packages with dependency map). Run after /lf-new-feature once the UX/UI team has shared Figma screen URLs.

skills.name: lf-design-system
skills.description: Connects to Figma via MCP Server, extracts typography, colors, spacing, border radius, shadows and all design token definitions, and generates specs/design-system.md — the official design system source of truth for the project. Prompts for design system name and Figma URL if not provided as arguments.
